01 / 07
Yucatecan

Luis Rufino

27 — 30 May 2026
"Where the Yucatán Peninsula meets classical France — in every single bite."

Born in Mérida, Yucatán, Luis spent 12 years forging deep ties with local fishermen and the bold flavors of the peninsula. A chapter as gastronomic advisor at Les 500 Diables in France gave his cooking its classical backbone — fused now with the rooted, sun-drenched spirit of Yucatecan cuisine.

Per Person
$75 / person
• Limited Seats
02 / 07
Mexican

Daniele Gonzalez

24 — 27 June 2026

"Born in Oaxaca. Trained in Mexico City. Arrived in Beirut with fire and mole and no intention of holding back."

Daniele builds menus that trace the full arc of Mexican cuisine — from the ancient to the radical. Fire-centered, smoke-forward, and unapologetically bold, her cooking draws on Oaxacan mole traditions, Yucatán street culture, and a decade spent refining technique in Mexico City's top kitchens. At Good Thing, she brings the heat of the plancha and the depth of a cuisine that has never been fully explored in Beirut.

● Booking opens soon — stay tuned
03 / 07
Japanese

Philipp Walther

July 2026

"Twenty years of obsessive study — and a lightness of touch that takes a lifetime to earn."

Philipp spent two decades mastering Japan's most meditative culinary traditions — kaiseki in Kyoto, fermentation studies in Osaka, ramen craft in Fukuoka. His menus are quiet, precise, and deeply considered. Each course arrives as a statement of restraint: nothing excess, nothing missing. In Beirut, he brings this philosophy intact — Japanese discipline redrawn with Levantine light.

● Booking opens soon — stay tuned
04 / 07
Spanish

Robert

August 2026

"The fearless generosity of the Spanish table — and a belief that every meal should feel like it will never end."

Chef Robert brings Spain's most defining quality to Beirut: abundance without waste, warmth without carelessness. His menus draw from the Catalan coast and the Andalusian interior — the plancha's heat, the complexity of aged sherry, the ritual of sharing. Every plate is designed to provoke conversation and demand another glass. A summer dinner with the volume turned all the way up.

● Booking opens soon — stay tuned
05 / 07
Norwegian

Joanne

6 — 13 September 2026

"Rooted in the Norwegian landscape — every ingredient carries the memory of where it came from."

Raised on a farm in Norway, Joanne Brynildsen Sørheim learned to cook before she learned to talk about it. She built a small company with her mother and sister sourcing organic produce from their land to the kitchens of Oslo and Hamar — a supply chain defined by distance measured in minutes, not miles. Her cooking is honest, seasonal, and deeply Nordic: foraged herbs, cold-water fish, root vegetables pulled that morning. In Beirut, she brings a northern quietness that feels wholly new.

● Booking opens soon — stay tuned
06 / 07
Nordic

Bjorn

October 2026

"Only what the season offers. Only what the land can give. A dinner that asks you to slow down."

From the forests and coastlines of Scandinavia, Chef Bjorn arrives with a philosophy of radical restraint. His menus are built entirely around what is available — cured, fermented, smoked, and preserved with the quiet intensity that defines the Nordic table at its best. In summer Beirut, he reinterprets this discipline through local produce and a respect for the ingredient above all else. A rare and considered evening.

● Booking opens soon — stay tuned
07 / 07
Levantine

Ben Blum

November 2026

"Levantine flavours rooted in memory — cooked with the precision of someone who never forgot where they came from."

Chicago-based Ben has built his career around the soul food of the Levant. As Executive Chef of Fiya — and before that at Michelin-listed SIFR, which earned three stars from the Chicago Tribune — he refined a cooking language that honours Middle Eastern heritage without nostalgia. His food is alive, rigorous, and deeply personal. Bringing him to Beirut, where the flavours he cooks with originate, feels like a homecoming.

● Booking opens soon — stay tuned
